era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty IV) continues. Khafre's reign is traditionally dated to c. 2558–2532 BCE, so this year may fall within or near his reign, depending on the chronological system used. Mesopotamian city-states are engaged in territorial disputes and irrigation management. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its mature phase. This era is characterized by the development of complex state systems, monumental architecture, and long-distance trade networks.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of the world outside Egypt, Mesopotamia, and the Indus Valley
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year in any known source tradition. The assignment of this year to Khafre's reign is speculative and depends on contested chronologies.
historiographic_note: The historical record for this year is entirely absent. No contemporary sources document specific events in 3155 BCE. Knowledge of this period comes from archaeological evidence (monumental remains, settlement patterns, pottery) and from king lists compiled centuries or millennia later. The chronology of the Old Kingdom is uncertain by ±50–100 years or more. For most of the world, no written records exist for this period.
